column1: Squarehead Technology leter etter Test Automation & CI Engineer i Oslo
column3: At Squarehead, we develop acoustic sensors capable of everything from detecting drones to providing full control in demanding security and industrial environments. In short: we give people super-hearing and situational awareness when it matters most. Weâ€™re now looking for new teammates who enjoy variety, learning, and building systems that matter. About the role Our main product is an array of 100+ tiny microphones, which, combined with some clever math and signal processing, enables us to zoom in on sound, and turn down the volume of the surroundings of the source of interest. In this way, we can for instance detect, locate and classify threats like drones, be it in the field or in a busy city. We're looking for a test automation and CI engineer to join our small, focused infrastructure team. Your mission: make it easy and reliable for our product teams to build, test, and ship code all the way from host-side unit tests to integration tests running on embedded hardware. Our CI system is built around GitLab, but much of the test automation infrastructure is still being shaped. This is a greenfield opportunity where you'll have real influence over how we do CI and testing going forward. What You'll Do Design, maintain, and optimize CI/CD pipelines in GitLab Build and evolve test automation frameworks for a C++ codebase with a TypeScript/React frontend Set up and manage on-device test infrastructure across embedded Linux platforms Improve build times, reliability, and developer feedback loops Collaborate with product teams to define test strategies and help them write effective tests What we're looking for Experience with CI/CD systems (GitLab CI preferred) Experience with test frameworks (e.g. Google Test, Jest, pytest, or similar) Comfortable with Linux, including embedded Linux environments Experience with collecting statistics and building dashboard for the developers Scripting skills (Bash, Python, or similar) Good written and spoken English is required Itâ€™s also nice if you have Familiarity with C++ build systems and toolchains Experience with Docker and containerized build/test environments Cross-compilation and embedded toolchains (For instance, we use Yocto) Hardware-in-the-loop testing A relevant degree (bachelor's or master's) is an advantage, but practical experience matters more. Why Squarehead Those of us who currently work here tend to appreciate: The product is genuinely cool. Acoustic drone detection isn't something you find at most companies. Small team, real impact. You work closely with developers and product managers with minimal overhead and maximum autonomy. We invest in developer experience by building our own tools to improve how we work. Everything is built here. Software and hardware, developed locally in our Oslo office. Occasional field trips to test systems in real-world conditions. Good work/life balance with a flexible workday. Working from home when needed is not a problem. Growth opportunities through conferences, courses, and hackathons. We're actively involved in organizing NDC TechTown and Oslo C++ Users Group. In addition to the points mentioned above, we have: Easy commute right next to the subway Subsidised canteen Weekly organised physical activity (currently padel tennis and climbing) Rooftop terrace for breaks and summer parties Indoor bicycle and car parking Wardrobe with showers Practical information We review applications and invite candidates for interviews on an ongoing basis, so if youâ€™re interested, we recommend applying soon. We work with partners who require confidentiality and have security requirements that we must ensure our employees can comply with. This position may require security clearance.

We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. Job Description As an Electrical QA/QC Engineer, you will be responsible for delivering the Electrical Quality Management System. As part of the QAQC Team, you will be responsible for the quality delivery of projects & equipment, ensuring that all client specifications are met, through inspections & walkdowns. You will work closely with the Quality Lead as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting-edge projects while ensuring that all client specifications are met. Your responsibilities will include: Overseeing the applicable Jones Engineering database for each project, ensuring it is kept up to date and accurate, which will essentially deliver the handover documentation to the client. Review benchmarks and First of Kind Installations Completion of system and equipment test packs using Project Quality Management Software on a project-by-project basis Liaise with the QA Lead on handover documentation, system handover and tagging dates. Liaise with the construction teams regarding handover dates, scheduling walk downs, developing snag lists etc. identifying snag trends and escalating to QA Leads. Review Installations for Compliance to Jones Project Specific Quality Plan & ITPs, Project standards, specification, design, procedures & expectations. Oversee 3rd party inspections & contractors to ensure project requirements are met. Ensure QA requirements are met by subcontractors. Oversee & complete regular site walkdowns & inspections. Travel required for Ireland and Mainland Europe site support where necessary. Attend quality meetings with management and subcontractors and report on quality as required. Liaise with other in-house specialist Electrical QA/QC Leads& Engineers as required. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to Data Centre, life sciences, construction, and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: Relevant Electrical/quality qualification. Electrical Trade background would be advantageous. Minimum 1+ year experience in a similar role desirable. Fluent English essential. Knowledge of electrical Systems and Schematics essential. Knowledge of Microsoft Office essential. Experience working with Quality software would be advantageous. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills essential. What this role offers: They are offering a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs.

column1: Trener Robotics (formerly T-ROBOTICS) leter etter QA Engineer i Trondheim
column3: Location: On-site in San Jose, California or Trondheim, Norway About Us Freshly backed by some of the top VCs in Silicon Valley, Trener is redefining robotics by combining advanced AI for intuitive programming with pre-trained skill models that are experts at industrial tasks. With offices in San Jose (USA) and Trondheim (Norway), we provide software that gives our users unprecedented ease-of-use and capabilities for their robots â€” allowing them to think, adapt, and perform complex tasks like any skilled worker. Our mission is to make industrial robotics intelligent, accessible, and deployable at scale â€” for manufacturers, integrators, and operators who need it to simply work. We are a dynamic team of engineers, roboticists, and technologists who are passionate about bringing AI-powered automation to the factory floor. About The Job The Release Engineering & Quality Assurance team is responsible for delivering releases of Trener's software platform at high velocity without sacrificing trust in the quality of each release. As a QA Engineer on this team, your job is to execute and review the tests that demonstrate this quality. When a test cannot be completed, you will document detailed steps for reproducing the error and work with the engineering team to verify that the issue has been resolved. As our scope grows, you will use your software development experience and your deepening knowledge of Trener's platform to convert manual tests into automated tests â€” helping us scale quality alongside our product. Responsibilities Perform manual tests of Trenerâ€™s software, both in simulation and on real robotics hardware workcells. Investigate and document failures, and assist the engineering team with verifying fixes. Develop manual test plans into automated tests in order to scale our quality engineering processes. Support the release engineering team in delivering high-quality, high-velocity software releases Requirements Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Robotics, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related field Excellent written communication skills and strong attention to details. You will need to concisely describe deviations from expected behavior and provide accurate steps to reproduce errors found during testing. Programming experience with Linux shell scripting and Python, specifically ROS2 Experience with 6-DOF robot arms from major manufacturers (ABB, KUKA, Fanuc, Universal Robots, or similar) What Will Differentiate You Master's degree in relevant engineering discipline Technical writing and/or presentation experience. Have you published projects with thorough, well-structured documentation? We would love to see. What we offer A unique opportunity to work at the forefront of robotics and AI A central role in a growing startup where your contributions will directly shape our future Competitive salary, employee equity, and bonuses A dynamic and international work environment with offices in the US and Norway

column1: CTS Group leter etter QA E&P Engineer - Project i Narvik
column3: QA E&P Engineer We are looking for a QA E&P Engineer to implement and enforce quality assurance and quality control for all Electrical & Power systems on a hyperscale data center project. You will ensure installations meet client specifications, international standards, and commissioning requirements, supporting defect-free, fully traceable, and resilient power infrastructure. What Youâ€™ll Do You will lead QA/QC activities for all electrical and power systems, ensuring compliance with drawings, specifications, and approved methods. You will conduct inspections, audits, and surveillance, manage quality documentation, oversee testing and commissioning readiness, and support phased energization handovers. You will identify non-conformances, track NCRs, and collaborate with construction, commissioning, and client teams to maintain quality and safety standards. Key Responsibilities Include Implementing and managing QA/QC plans for Electrical & Power systems Conducting inspections, audits, and routine quality surveillance Reviewing and approving Electrical ITPs, check sheets, and test procedures Overseeing installation quality for MV/LV distribution, UPS, generators, switchgear, transformers, earthing, cabling, and terminations Ensuring correct installation, labeling, and system identification Supporting FAT, SAT, pre-commissioning, and energization readiness Maintaining full traceability of materials, test reports, and as-built documentation Identifying defects, managing NCRs, and supporting snag resolution Ensuring QA activities comply with HSE requirements and promoting a quality-driven, safety-first culture Reporting on quality status and metrics to project leadership and coordinating with stakeholders Who Weâ€™re Looking For You hold a degree or HND in Electrical Engineering or a related discipline and have proven experience in QA/QC for electrical and power systems, ideally in data centers, mission-critical, pharmaceutical, or high-tech projects. You have strong knowledge of electrical standards, testing, and commissioning processes, with excellent documentation control, attention to detail, and experience in fast-track, phased energization environments. Qualifications & Skills Degree / HND in Electrical Engineering or related discipline QA/QC experience on Electrical & Power systems in large-scale projects Knowledge of electrical standards, testing, and commissioning processes Experience with fast-track, phased energization projects Strong documentation, inspection, and reporting skills Familiarity with digital QA/QC platforms and client commissioning processes Excellent attention to detail, problem-solving, and communication skills Fluency in English (written and spoken) Desirable: ISO 9001 Internal Auditor, CQI/IRCA certification, CSCS / Safe Pass Why Join Us You will play a critical role in delivering high-quality electrical and power systems across complex data center projects, ensuring safe, compliant, and resilient infrastructure while supporting commissioning and phased handovers.

Kibana or OpenSearch Dashboards. Discipline, a responsible approach to work, and, of course, curiosity. Team building activities, corporate events. Corporate daycare for employees. Shares in the company.

column1: Visual Components Rekrytointi Software QA Engineer
column3: Get to know Visual Components At Visual Components, we build world-class 3D simulation software for manufacturers. Our solution can be used for designing the factories of the future already today. By using our software, the user can design production lines, simulate and analyse the production flow, perform virtual commissioning projects, and much more. Today our solution is trusted by more than 2400 organizations in the manufacturing industry worldwide, starting from small local manufacturers and to huge automotive corporations. Visual Components is an international company. We are headquartered in Espoo, Finland, but we have offices in Tampere Finland, the US, and Germany, along with a global partner network of partners and resellers. The Visual Components team is truly multinational - we have people from over 22 countries, and we speak at least 12 languages! The role and the team We are looking for a Software Test Engineer to join our QA Team and to reinforce our team working on renewing the Visual Components platform and building future-proof features that will enable our product to grow. The team members work as members of the software development teams and have their own QA teamâ€™s development goals. The QA team members share tasks to encourage learning in different areas and to make the team(s) more adaptable. The software development teams use Scrum and comprise of developers, a testing engineer, and a product owner. Your impact The main tasks in our QA are: Manual testing and feature verification. Developing scripts and test data for e.g., UI test automation or our other tooling. Developing testing practices and collaborating with developers. Software developers will still be writing their unit tests and other test automation. Being a part of our Licensing development team, you will be tasked with testing complex and interconnected licensing systems, both on desktop and on the web. You will be responsible for helping the team maintain and improve their quality standards and work with them to tackle various challenges. The skills you will bring We are looking for someone who has strong problem-solving skills, great soft skills, an analytical mindset with the ability to reveal the root causes for complex issues. You will succeed if you have the willingness and attitude to learn a new business domain and deepen their skills in testing and using test automation technology. What counts, is passion for learning and developing! Skills important for this role: Experience working with and testing complex legacy systems. Experience testing API with tools like Postman. Experience in testing and collaborating across the full web stack, including both frontend and backend components. Strong process improvement mindset with an emphasis on short and long term change. Excellent communication skills with the ability to work effectively across teams. Deep logical thinking: the ability to decompose complex problems, find the root causes of issues, and suggest solutions based on the available data. English language - fluency in speaking and writing in English because it is our main working language Beneficial: Experience working with Cypress. Beneficial: Experience with a variety of licensing systems. Beneficial: C# and Python We appreciate relevant educational background e.g., a bachelorâ€™s degree in automation technology, robotics, electrical engineering, computer science, or information technology - but this is not mandatory in case you have learned the skills some other way. Perks of joining us The opportunity to further develop and expand your skills and make an impact to our own platform. Flexible working times and remote work. An employer who supports in various life situations. Safe and stable workplace. Competitive salary with comprehensive personnel benefits, e.g., learning platforms, extensive occupational health care and health insurance, lunch benefits, sport & cultural benefit vouchers. At Visual Components, our most valuable assets are our employees. We treat everyone with honesty and transparency, help each other, and do our work with passion. We also enjoy spending time together even after work. Pool tournaments, game nights, and after works and sports activities are part of our life as a team. How to apply Please send your application in English via the form on our career page. We cannot accept applications sent to us via email or private messages due to the GDPR. We will hire a person as soon as we find the right candidate. Next steps with us Screening is within 4 weeks of receiving your application Introductory interview with our Talent Acquisition specialist Online interview with the QA Team Manager Final interview with the manager and the team Hiring manager In case you have any specific questions about the role, feel free to contact QA Team Manager, lenny.smith@visualcomponents.com

column1: GE HealthCare Rekrytointi Intern â€“ Test automation frameworks

column3: Company Overview NACOS Marine is a globally integrated group of companies at the forefront of maritime automation, navigation, and control systems. Building on decades of expertise and a heritage of trusted solutions, NACOS Marine continues to pioneer technologies that set the standard for safer, smarter, and more efficient ship operations worldwide. Our engineering teams develop advanced maritime solutions where reliability, stability, and quality are critical for successful deployment and long-term operation. Role Overview We are seeking a Test Engineer (m/f/d) to support system validation, hardware and software testing, and release readiness of our products. In this hands-on role, you will work independently while collaborating closely with engineering teams to ensure high product quality. You will be responsible for writing and executing test cases, performing functional and integration testing, and supporting system setup and troubleshooting in lab and test environments. The position combines manual testing, hardware validation, and system-level testing in a practical engineering environment. Key Responsibilities Testing & Quality Assurance Design, execute, and maintain test cases, test plans, and test scripts Perform functional, regression, integration, and system testing Conduct release testing, FAT testing, and system validation Identify, log, and track defects using Azure DevOps Support test automation readiness and improve testing processes System Setup & Configuration Install and configure Windows and Linux systems Set up PCs, servers, and test environments in lab environments Perform firmware installations, upgrades, and validation Support hardware setup, configuration, and diagnostics Troubleshooting & Integration Testing Diagnose hardware and software issues and support resolution Support hardware testing and integration testing activities Work with virtual machines and hypervisors for test environments Assist in maintaining lab setups for hardware and software testing Documentation & Reporting Document test results, environments, and findings Report product quality status and release readiness Support continuous improvement of testing processes Key Requirements Degree in Computer Science, IT, Electronics, or equivalent experience Hands-on experience in software, system, or hardware testing Knowledge of functional and regression testing Experience with Windows and Linux environments Familiarity with Azure DevOps and virtual environments Practical, structured, and independent working style Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills Fluent in English Why choose us and why weâ€™d choose you At NACOS Marine, you will work in a practical and hands-on engineering environment, contributing to the quality and reliability of advanced maritime solutions. You will collaborate with experienced engineering teams and gain exposure to system-level testing, integration, and release validation. We offer a dynamic, international working environment where quality and innovation go hand in hand.

column1: AlphaSense Rekrytointi Head of Engineering Productivity & Experience
column3: About AlphaSense The worldâ€™s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clientsâ€™ own research content. The acquisition of Tegus by AlphaSense in 2024 advances our shared mission to empower professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence. Together, AlphaSense and Tegus will accelerate growth, innovation, and content expansion, with complementary product and content capabilities that enable users to unearth even more comprehensive insights from thousands of content sets. Our platform is trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland. Come join us! The Role We are looking for a Head of Engineering Productivity & Experience to lead the evolution of how software is built, tested, and delivered at AlphaSense. This is a high-impact, Director-level leadership role shaping developer experience and engineering productivity across our global ~400-engineer organization through platforms, tooling, and engineering practices. The position will initially lead teams of ~13 engineers while defining the operating model and long-term trajectory of the Engineering Productivity domain. Your mission will be to drive the strategy for a unified platform ecosystem that bridges AI-driven development, Developer Tooling, Web Infrastructure, and QA Automation. You will lead the evolution of platforms that enable high-confidence releases across local development, microfrontend delivery, and automated quality engineering, while shaping a seamless developer-facing CI/CD experience in partnership with SRE and Cloud Platform teams. A core pillar of this mission is advancing the AI-assisted SDLC. You will define and implement AI-enabled tooling and workflows that measurably improve developer productivity and experience. This includes areas such as AI-assisted code reviews, test generation and coverage expansion, developer documentation, and improving the entire inner-loop development experience. The goal is to ensure AI becomes a practical accelerator in everyday engineering workflows rather than a standalone tool. While this role is centered on organizational strategy and platform leadership, we value "player-coach" DNA. You should be a technically sharp leader who is comfortable diving into architectural reviews or getting close to the code when necessary to unblock teams or accelerate progress. We are primarily seeking Director-level candidates, though strong Senior Engineering Managers with a proven track record of scaling DevEx or Platform organizations are also encouraged to apply. What Youâ€™ll Do Lead developer productivity and experience across AlphaSenseâ€™s engineering organization Drive the evolution of developer productivity tooling, covering inner-loop development workflows Define and implement a strategy for AI-driven developer productivity capabilities across SDLC Own the developer-facing CI/CD experience, improving build, test, and deployment workflows Build and scale QA automation platforms that enable high confidence in software delivery Lead and scale web infrastructure and microfrontend delivery, enabling independent releases Improve engineering velocity by reducing friction in development, testing, and delivery workflows Partner with SRE, Cloud, and Product Engineering teams to improve the software delivery Establish metrics and feedback loops to improve developer productivity and experience Who You Are Several years of engineering leadership in developer productivity and experience Strong understanding of the software development lifecycle and modern engineering workflows Experience improving CI/CD developer experience Experience building or scaling internal developer platforms Experience driving test automation and quality engineering practices at scale Experience operating in large engineering organizations with distributed teams Demonstrated experience building and mentoring high-performing engineering teams Experience implementing AI-assisted development tooling Ability to lead through influence and collaboration across multiple engineering organizations Experience improving engineering metrics such as delivery velocity, cycle time, and CSAT Nice To Have Experience with progressive delivery Familiarity with cloud-native architectures and Kubernetes-based platforms EST or UK timezone for closer overlap with direct team members Familiarity with modern frontend architectures and microfrontend delivery models What We Offer Playing a part in the story of a fast growing company Interesting career opportunities and training Relocation support for single employees, couples and families While we do have an office in Helsinki, weâ€™re a distributed company. We value outcomes over time, and offer flexible working arrangements. We usually meet at the office once a week. Private healthcare services from Terveystalo 2 weeks of vacation granted up front, and possibility to use vacation days as they accrue Access to gym room, gaming room, free parking near the office, mobile phone, Lunch benefit, mobile plan, home internet, remote work equipment, and many more.... You can apply by sending your cover letter and resume through the application form. AlphaSense is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to a work environment that supports, inspires, and respects all individuals. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling AlphaSenseâ€™s commitment to equal employment opportunity. AlphaSense does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of race, color, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, religion, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, military or veteran status, disability, or any other non-merit factor. This policy applies to every aspect of employment at AlphaSense, including recruitment, hiring, training, advancement, and termination. In addition, it is the policy of AlphaSense to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where a particular employee works. Recruiting Scams and Fraud We At AlphaSense Have Been Made Aware Of Fraudulent Job Postings And Individuals Impersonating AlphaSense Recruiters. These Scams May Involve Fake Job Offers, Requests For Sensitive Personal Information, Or Demands For Payment. Please Note AlphaSense never asks candidates to pay for job applications, equipment, or training. All official communications will come from an @alpha-sense.com email address. If youâ€™re unsure about a job posting or recruiter, verify it on our Careers page. If you believe youâ€™ve been targeted by a scam or have any doubts regarding the authenticity of any job listing purportedly from or on behalf of AlphaSense please contact us.
